strengths: Modular architecture with separated concerns (tools, skills, cloud, db) reduces blast radius of compromises
Vector memory (sqlite-vec) and SQLite persistence provide local control over sensitive data without mandatory cloud sync
Permission/path validation layer (permissions.ts) exists to restrict filesystem access
Windows-native SEA packaging reduces dependency on third-party runtimes and external package managers
Discrete tool implementations in src/tools allow granular capability auditing and least-privilege enforcement
Kill-switch mechanism (src/runtime, src/utils) provides emergency termination capability
weaknesses: Command execution in runner.ts uses execSync without mandatory isDangerousCommand validation; injection risk if callers unsanitized
missions/ directory unreadable (90s timeout); unverified mission logic could execute arbitrary commands or exfiltrate data
permissions.ts falls back to process.cwd() when WORKSPACE_ROOT unset; allows unintended filesystem traversal
Hardcoded user paths (C:\Users\angel\...) in scripts/a4_run.ts expose developer environment; breaks portability and reveals system structure
Kill-switch PowerShell subprocess spawned without timeout/cleanup; potential zombie processes consuming resources
No CI/CD or automated test framework; manual console-based testing cannot catch regressions or injection vulnerabilities at scale
recommendations: Wrap all execSync calls in src/utils/runner.ts with mandatory isDangerousCommand() check; reject execution if check fails or is missing
Scan missions/ directory contents immediately; validate all mission config files contain only declarative data (JSON schema validation); block imperative code execution
Update permissions.ts to require explicit WORKSPACE_ROOT environment variable; remove process.cwd() fallback or log warning before using it
Replace hardcoded paths in scripts/a4_run.ts with environment variables (e.g., %USERPROFILE% or explicit config); add path normalization/validation
Add timeout (5s) and cleanup handlers to kill_switch PowerShell spawning in src/runtime; use child_process.spawn with explicit termination on timeout
Implement GitHub Actions CI pipeline to run automated test suite (convert manual scripts to Jest/Mocha); add SAST scanning (ESLint security rules, npm audit) on every PR

combined_recommendations: Wrap all execSync calls in src/utils/runner.ts with mandatory isDangerousCommand() check; reject execution if check fails or is missing
Scan missions/ directory contents immediately; validate all mission config files contain only declarative data (JSON schema validation); block imperative code execution
Update permissions.ts to require explicit WORKSPACE_ROOT environment variable; remove process.cwd() fallback or log warning before using it
Replace hardcoded paths in scripts/a4_run.ts with environment variables (e.g., %USERPROFILE% or explicit config); add path normalization/validation
Add timeout (5s) and cleanup handlers to kill_switch PowerShell spawning in src/runtime; use child_process.spawn with explicit termination on timeout
Implement GitHub Actions CI pipeline with automated tests (Jest/Mocha) and SAST scanning (ESLint security rules, npm audit) on every PR
